Okay, let's try to figure out when William could have gone to the amusement park. 

First, let's list out all the information given:

1. William woke up at 5am.
2. Betty saw William walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 5am to 9am.
3. David saw William reading at the library from 4pm to 7pm.
4. Lisa saw William taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 7pm to 9pm.
5. The amusement park was closed after 9pm.

The question is asking between what times William could have gone to the amusement park.

Let me think step by step:

From 5am to 9am, William was at the Statue of Liberty according to Betty. So he couldn't go to the amusement park during that time.

From 4pm to 7pm, William was at the library reading according to David. So he couldn't go to the amusement park during that time either.

From 7pm to 9pm, William was taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa according to Lisa. So he couldn't go to the amusement park during that time either.

The amusement park was closed after 9pm, so William couldn't have gone after 9pm.

Now, let's look at the time between when William woke up (5am) and when he was seen at the Statue of Liberty (5am-9am). So between 5am and 9am, he was at the Statue of Liberty.

After 9am, there's no information about where William was until 4pm when David saw him at the library. So between 9am and 4pm, there is no evidence that William was doing anything else. Therefore, he could have gone to the amusement park during this time.

Now let's check the answer options:

(A) 9am to 4pm - This is the time when there are no known activities, so this is a possible time for William to have gone to the amusement park.
(B) 5am to 9am - William was at the Statue of Liberty during this time, so this is not possible.
(C) 4pm to 7pm - William was at the library during this time, so this is not possible.
(D) 7pm to 9pm - William was at the Leaning Tower of Pisa during this time, so this is not possible.
